2606.14445.title: tap: A File-Based Protocol for Heterogeneous LLM Agent Collaboration
2606.14445.paper_summary: Existing multi-agent software development systems have proposed many forms of agent collaboration, including role-based collaboration and automated code review. However, many systems assume a common runtime, a central conversation server, or the same API family. Under these assumptions, LLM agents from different vendors cannot easily exchange messages directly from their own execution environments while dividing development and review work on a shared codebase. This paper presents tap, a file-based collaboration protocol that allows Claude (Anthropic) and Codex (OpenAI) to collaborate on one codebase without shared memory or an identical runtime. The core of tap is a file-first design that preserves markdown files with metadata as original messages, combines a file inspection path (file communication, Tier 1) with real-time notification paths for Claude and Codex (real-time communication, Tier 2), and isolates work through separate git worktrees. Even if real-time notification fails or a receiver restarts, the message file remains available and the same content can be inspected again. In a 27-day, 37-generation self-applied operation where tap was used to develop and review itself, we collected 209 tap-related pull requests and 717 operational artifacts. An analysis of 375 review artifacts showed that the share of reviews recording at least one defect or requested change was 69.8% for heterogeneous model pairs and 53.1% for homogeneous model pairs. These results show that tap, which combines file-based message preservation with real-time notification, operates in a real production repository, and that combining heterogeneous models and execution environments can broaden review perspectives. tap is distributed as the open-source npm package @hua-labs/tap (v0.5.2).
2606.14445.paper_summary_zh: 現有的多代理軟體開發系統提出了多種形式的代理協作，包括基於角色的協作和自動化代碼審查。<lb/>然而，許多系統假設有一個共同的執行環境、一個中央對話伺服器或相同的 API 家族。<lb/>在這些假設下，來自不同供應商的 LLM 代理無法輕易地從各自的執行環境中直接交換消息，同時在共享代碼庫上分工進行開發和審查。<lb/>本文介紹了 tap，一種基於文件的協作協議，允許 Claude（Anthropic）和 Codex（OpenAI）在沒有共享記憶體或相同執行環境的情況下協作於一個代碼庫。<lb/>tap 的核心是一種以文件為先的設計，保留帶有元數據的 markdown 文件作為原始消息，將文件檢查路徑（文件通信，第一級）與 Claude 和 Codex 的實時通知路徑（實時通信，第二級）相結合，並通過單獨的 git 工作樹來隔離工作。<lb/>即使實時通知失敗或接收者重新啟動，消息文件仍然可用，且相同的內容可以再次檢查。<lb/>在一個為期 27 天、37 代的自我應用操作中，使用 tap 進行開發和審查，我們收集了 209 個與 tap 相關的拉取請求和 717 個操作工件。<lb/>對 375 個審查工件的分析顯示，記錄至少一個缺陷或請求變更的審查所佔比例為異構模型對的 69.8% 和同構模型對的 53.1%。<lb/>這些結果顯示，tap 結合了基於文件的消息保留與實時通知，能夠在真實的生產庫中運行，並且結合異構模型和執行環境可以擴大審查的視角。<lb/>tap 作為開源 npm 套件 @hua-labs/tap (v0.5.2) 發布。
2606.14445.author: Minseo Kim et.al.
